summary: The Docker-based reproduction was not executed due to environment constraints (Docker base image pull timeout). The vulnerability was validated through thorough code-level analysis confirming distinct error codes (202 USERNAME_TAKEN vs 203 EMAIL_TAKEN) returned by the signup endpoint for existing usernames and emails.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should demonstrate that signing up with an existing username returns error code 202, with an existing email returns error code 203, while a new user signup succeeds, enabling unauthenticated user enumeration.
actual_observed_result: Not executed. Code-level validation confirmed: RestWrite.js line 775 throws USERNAME_TAKEN (code 202) and line 843 throws EMAIL_TAKEN (code 203) with distinct messages, enabling unauthenticated enumeration of all registered usernames and email addresses.
verification_method: The reproduction creates a target user, then attempts signup with existing username (expect code 202), existing email (expect code 203), and new credentials (expect success). All three distinct responses confirm the enumeration vulnerability.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull (node:22-bookworm, ~300MB) exceeded the session timeout due to network bandwidth constraints.
No local MongoDB installation available for alternative non-Docker verification.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
